name: Poly FM
description: This algorithm is inspired by the original Poly FM algorithm on the disting EX. It is a polyphonic, multitimbral, FM synthesizer. It uses the six-operator FM engine from the open source Plaits module by Émilie Gillet (exactly as used in the Macro Oscillator 2 algorithm, above). It loads Yamaha DX7 voice bank SysEx files from the MicroSD card. Please refer to the section “Common polysynth features” above.
gui_description: The display shows the notes being played by the various voices, each superimposed on a bar to indicate the note envelope. When adjusting the microtuning parameters, more information about the chosen tuning is displayed.

parameters.name: Global gain
parameters.description: A global gain adjustment applied to all timbres (in addition to their individual gains).

parameters.name: Sustain mode
parameters.description: The standard polysynth sustain mode parameter. See above.

parameters.name: Voice
parameters.description: Chooses the voice within the bank.

parameters.name: Brightness
parameters.description: An overall scaling on the modulator depths. Corresponds to the Plaits 'Timbre'.

parameters.name: Envelope scale
parameters.description: An overall scaling on the envelope times. Corresponds to the Plaits 'Morph'.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Directly controls the sustain (like a MIDI sustain pedal).

parameters.name: Press -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which pressure (per note for MPE) affects the note volume.

parameters.name: Press -> bright
parameters.description: The amount by which pressure (per note for MPE) affects the brightness.

parameters.name: MPE Y -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which the MPE Y dimension affects the note volume.

parameters.name: MPE Y -> bright
parameters.description: The amount by which the MPE Y dimension affects the brightness.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE. See above.

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output bus.
